perturbation.original_text: The Fund hereby appoints [●], and [●] hereby accepts such appointment, as the exclusive Remarketing Agent of the VRRM-MFP Shares for the Variable Rate Remarketed Mode for the purpose of establishing on each Business Day the Dividend Rate in respect of the VRRM-MFP Shares and, in connection with a tender, remarketing such VRRM-MFP Shares on behalf of the Beneficial Owners or Holders thereof, as applicable, and calculating the Purchase Price therefor, among other things; and performing such other duties as are assigned to the Remarketing Agent in the Supplement, all pursuant to the procedures set forth in the Supplement and this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Fund hereby appoints [●], and [●] hereby accepts such appointment, as a non-exclusive Remarketing Agent of the VRRM-MFP Shares for the Variable Rate Remarketed Mode for the purpose of establishing on each Business Day the Dividend Rate in respect of the VRRM-MFP Shares and, in connection with a tender, remarketing such VRRM-MFP Shares on behalf of the Beneficial Owners or Holders thereof, as applicable, and calculating the Purchase Price therefor, among other things; and performing such other duties as are assigned to the Remarketing Agent in the Supplement, all pursuant to the procedures set forth in the Supplement and this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'exclusive' to 'non-exclusive' creates an in-text contradiction. While Section 2(a) now states that the Remarketing Agent is non-exclusive, other sections of the agreement may implicitly or explicitly assume exclusivity, particularly in clauses detailing obligations and notices only applicable to 'the Remarketing Agent.' For example, Section 5(b) discusses the appointment of a *successor* remarketing agent, implying the need to replace a single entity, rather than adding to a pool of agents. This contradiction leads to uncertainty regarding responsibilities and rights. The change that introduces the perturbation is in Section 2, however there are sections where the contradiction impacts, such as Section 5(b)
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) The Fund may remove the Remarketing Agent with respect to the VRRM-MFP Shares by giving at least 60 days' prior written notice to the Remarketing Agent (and will provide prior notice also to the Calculation and Paying Agent, if any); provided, however, that no such removal shall become effective for an additional 30 days unless the Fund shall have appointed at least one nationally recognized securities dealer with expertise in remarketing variable rate securities as a successor Remarketing Agent for the VRRM-MFP Shares and the successor Remarketing Agent shall have entered into a remarketing agreement with the Fund, in form and substance satisfactory to the Fund, in which it shall have agreed to, among other duties, conduct remarketings in respect of VRRM-MFP Shares and determine the Dividend Rate on each Business Day for the VRRM-MFP Shares in accordance with the terms and conditions of the Supplement. In each of the occurrences described in clause (a) or (b), the Fund shall use its best efforts to appoint a successor Remarketing Agent for such VRRM-MFP Shares and enter into a remarketing agreement with such Person as soon as reasonably practicable.
perturbation.justification: The change from "exclusive" to "non-exclusive" in the definition of the Remarketing Agent directly contradicts the assumption of a single agent in the Contradicted Text, particularly regarding the appointment of a "successor Remarketing Agent." The original text in Section 5(b) clearly implies the replacement of a single entity, whereas the changed text allows for multiple agents. The phrase 'successor Remarketing Agent' assumes there is only one to replace, which is not compatible with having multiple non-exclusive Remarketing Agents, thereby triggering an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Remarketing Agent shall keep such books and records with respect to the performance of its duties hereunder as shall be consistent with prudent industry practice and shall, to the extent permitted by law, make such books and records available for inspection by the Fund on reasonable notice during normal business hours. Any costs and expenses associated with such inspections shall be for the account of the party requesting such inspection.
perturbation.changed_text: The Remarketing Agent shall keep such books and records with respect to the performance of its duties hereunder as it deems necessary and shall not be required to make such books and records available for inspection by the Fund. Any requests for inspection will be subjected to internal approval by the Remarketing Agent.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity concerning the Remarketing Agent’s responsibilities and rights of the Fund, thus creating an in-text contradiction. By stating the Remarketing Agent only keeps books and records that it 'deems necessary,' this directly conflicts the idea that the Remarketing agent should be keepig books and records with "prudent industry practice". The change also restricts the Fund's access to these records, making oversight more difficult. This perturbs Section 11. Remarketing Agent's Performance; Duty of Care where Remarketing Agent's performance is to be determined solely by the express provisions of the agreement and the Supplement, and that the Fund in in bad faith may conclusively rely upon any document furnished to it. This creates uncertainty about the scope of the Remarketing Agent's obligations and the Fund's ability to monitor compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) The duties and obligations of the Remarketing Agent shall be determined solely by the express provisions of this Agreement and the Supplement. No implied covenants or obligations shall be read into this Agreement, or the Supplement. In the absence of bad faith on the part of the Remarketing Agent, the Remarketing Agent may conclusively rely upon any document furnished to it, which purports to conform to the requirements of this Agreement and the Statement, as to the truth of the statements expressed in any of such documents. The Remarketing Agent shall be protected in acting upon any document or communication reasonably believed by it to have been signed, presented or made by the proper party or parties. The Remarketing Agent shall incur no liability to the Fund, the Investment Adviser, the Calculation and Paying Agent or to any Beneficial Owner (or its Agent Member) or any Holder of the VRRM-MFP Shares in its individual capacity or as Remarketing Agent for any action or failure to act, in connection with its duties under this Agreement and the Supplement or otherwise, except as a result of bad faith, gross negligence or willful misconduct on its part.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction because it alters the Remarketing Agent's obligations concerning record-keeping and inspection, directly conflicting with Section (a) of the Contradicted Text. The original text mandated record-keeping consistent with 'prudent industry practice' and allowed Fund inspections with reasonable notice. The changed text grants the Remarketing Agent discretion over what records to keep ('deems necessary') and restricts Fund access, subjecting inspection requests to internal approval. This conflicts with Section (a), which states that the Remarketing Agent's duties are 'determined solely by the express provisions of this Agreement' and allows the Remarketing Agent to rely on furnished documents without liability, absent bad faith. The change creates ambiguity about the scope of obligations and the Fund's oversight rights, establishing a strong in-text contradiction that could lead to disputes over compliance.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be amended by any instrument in writing signed by all of the parties hereto so long as this Agreement as amended is not inconsistent with the Supplement in effect as of the date of any such amendment. The parties acknowledge that amendments to this Agreement (including with respect to Section 2(c)) are subject to prior notice requirements as set forth in the Tender and Paying Agent Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended unilaterally by the Fund with written notice to the Remarketing Agent. The parties acknowledge that amendments to this Agreement (including with respect to Section 2(c)) are not subject to prior notice requirements as set forth in the Tender and Paying Agent Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an internal contradiction concerning amendment procedures. By stating the Fund can unilaterally amend the agreement, there creates conflict with Section 12 which states 'Without the prior written consent of the Remarketing Agent, the Fund will not agree or consent to any amendment, supplement or modification of the Tender and Paying Agent Agreement, this Agreement or the Supplement, nor waive any provision thereof, if such amendment, supplement, modification or waiver would materially adversely affect the interests of the Remarketing Agent, in the Remarketing Agent's sole discretion'. The change also removes the requirement to follow 'prior notice requirements as set forth in the Tender and Paying Agent Agreement', conflicting with the original clause and introducing potential disputes over procedural adherence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without the prior written consent of the Remarketing Agent, the Fund will not agree or consent to any amendment, supplement or modification of the Tender and Paying Agent Agreement, this Agreement or the Supplement, nor waive any provision thereof, if such amendment, supplement, modification or waiver would materially adversely affect the interests of the Remarketing Agent, in the Remarketing Agent's sole discretion; provided, that, for purposes of this Section 12, any changes or amendments to the rating agency criteria provided in the Supplement for the VRRM-MFP Shares shall not be deemed to materially adversely affect the interests of the Remarketing Agent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Fund can unilaterally amend the agreement with written notice to the Remarketing Agent. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Fund cannot amend the agreement without the prior written consent of the Remarketing Agent if such amendment would materially adversely affect the interests of the Remarketing Agent. The unilateral amendment clause allows the Fund to bypass the Remarketing Agent's consent, creating a strong and direct conflict. The altered text introduces ambiguity and directly breaks the prior clause's compatibility requirement.
